content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Insert and verify one showcase fan-out item attempt.
3	set -euo pipefail
4	
5	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)"
6	ITEM_ID="${1:?usage: run_item_check.sh item-NN [attempt]}"
7	ATTEMPT="${2:-}"
8	
9	RUN_DIR="${RUN_DIR:?RUN_DIR must point at the fanout run directory}"
10	CORPUS="${CORPUS:?CORPUS must point at the pinned Scrapy checkout}"
11	ITEM="$RUN_DIR/items/$ITEM_ID.json"
12	
13	if [ -z "$ATTEMPT" ]; then
14	  ATTEMPT_DIR="$(find "$RUN_DIR/workers" -maxdepth 1 -type d -name "$ITEM_ID-attempt-*" | sort | tail -n 1)"
15	else
16	  ATTEMPT_DIR="$RUN_DIR/workers/$ITEM_ID-attempt-$ATTEMPT"
17	fi
18	
19	if [ -z "${ATTEMPT_DIR:-}" ] || [ ! -d "$ATTEMPT_DIR" ]; then
20	  echo "attempt directory not found for $ITEM_ID" >&2
21	  exit 2
22	fi
23	
24	RESPONSE="$ATTEMPT_DIR/response.json"
25	MODIFIED="$ATTEMPT_DIR/modified.py"
26	VERIFY="$ATTEMPT_DIR/verify.json"
27	
28	set +e
29	python3 "$ROOT/scripts/fanout/insert_docstrings.py" \
30	  --corpus "$CORPUS" \
31	  --item "$ITEM" \
32	  --response "$RESPONSE" \
33	  --out "$MODIFIED" >"$ATTEMPT_DIR/insert.stdout" 2>"$ATTEMPT_DIR/insert.stderr"
34	INSERT_RC=$?
35	set -e
36	
37	python3 "$ROOT/scripts/fanout/verify_item.py" \
38	  --corpus "$CORPUS" \
39	  --item "$ITEM" \
40	  --modified "$MODIFIED" \
41	  --out "$VERIFY" \
42	  --insertion-rc "$INSERT_RC"
43

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Insert and verify one showcase fan-out item attempt.
set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/../.." && pwd)"
ITEM_ID="${1:?usage: run_item_check.sh item-NN [attempt]}"
ATTEMPT="${2:-}"

RUN_DIR="${RUN_DIR:?RUN_DIR must point at the fanout run directory}"
CORPUS="${CORPUS:?CORPUS must point at the pinned Scrapy checkout}"
ITEM="$RUN_DIR/items/$ITEM_ID.json"

if [ -z "$ATTEMPT" ]; then
  ATTEMPT_DIR="$(find "$RUN_DIR/workers" -maxdepth 1 -type d -name "$ITEM_ID-attempt-*" | sort | tail -n 1)"
else
  ATTEMPT_DIR="$RUN_DIR/workers/$ITEM_ID-attempt-$ATTEMPT"
fi

if [ -z "${ATTEMPT_DIR:-}" ] || [ ! -d "$ATTEMPT_DIR" ]; then
  echo "attempt directory not found for $ITEM_ID" >&2
  exit 2
fi

RESPONSE="$ATTEMPT_DIR/response.json"
MODIFIED="$ATTEMPT_DIR/modified.py"
VERIFY="$ATTEMPT_DIR/verify.json"

set +e
python3 "$ROOT/scripts/fanout/insert_docstrings.py" \
  --corpus "$CORPUS" \
  --item "$ITEM" \
  --response "$RESPONSE" \
  --out "$MODIFIED" >"$ATTEMPT_DIR/insert.stdout" 2>"$ATTEMPT_DIR/insert.stderr"
INSERT_RC=$?
set -e

python3 "$ROOT/scripts/fanout/verify_item.py" \
  --corpus "$CORPUS" \
  --item "$ITEM" \
  --modified "$MODIFIED" \
  --out "$VERIFY" \
  --insertion-rc "$INSERT_RC"

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Verify one scratch-modified fan-out item without model judgment."""
3	
4	from __future__ import annotations
5	
6	import argparse
7	import ast
8	import pathlib
9	import re
10	import subprocess
11	import sys
12	from typing import Any
13	
14	SCRIPT_DIR = pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parent
15	if str(SCRIPT_DIR) not in sys.path:
16	    sys.path.insert(0, str(SCRIPT_DIR))
17	
18	from fanout_common import (  # noqa: E402
19	    item_file,
20	    params_for,
21	    parse_source,
22	    qualname_targets,
23	    read_json,
24	    strip_docstrings,
25	    write_json,
26	)
27	
28	PLACEHOLDER_RE = re.compile(r"\b(todo|tbd|fixme|placeholder)\b", re.IGNORECASE)
29	
30	
31	def fail(check: str, reason: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
32	    return {"passed": False, "failed_check": check, "reason": reason}
33	
34	
35	def ok() -> dict[str, Any]:
36	    return {"passed": True, "failed_check": None, "reason": None}
37	
38	
39	def compile_check(path: pathlib.Path) -> tuple[bool, str]:
40	    result = subprocess.run(
41	        [sys.executable, "-m", "py_compile", str(path)],
42	        capture_output=True,
43	        text=True,
44	    )
45	    return result.returncode == 0, (result.stdout + result.stderr).strip()
46	
47	
48	def word_present(text: str, name: str) -> bool:
49	    return re.search(rf"(?<![A-Za-z0-9_]){re.escape(name)}(?![A-Za-z0-9_])", text) is not None
50	
51	
52	def verify(corpus: pathlib.Path, item: dict[str, Any], modified_path: pathlib.Path, insertion_rc: int = 0) -> dict[str, Any]:
53	    if insertion_rc != 0:
54	        return fail("insertion_clean", f"insert_docstrings.py exited {insertion_rc}")
55	    passed_compile, compile_output = compile_check(modified_path)
56	    if not passed_compile:
57	        return fail("compiles", compile_output or "py_compile failed")
58	
59	    pristine_path = item_file(corpus, item)
60	    pristine_source = pristine_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
61	    modified_source = modified_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
62	    pristine_tree = parse_source(pristine_source, item["path"])
63	    modified_tree = parse_source(modified_source, str(modified_path))
64	    pristine_targets = qualname_targets(pristine_tree)
65	
66	    coverage_missing = []
67	    pristine_nodes = collect_nodes_multi(pristine_tree)
68	    modified_nodes = collect_nodes_multi(modified_tree)
69	    for qualname in item["targets"]:
70	        pairs = list(zip(pristine_nodes.get(qualname, []), modified_nodes.get(qualname, [])))
71	        missing_pairs = [pair for pair in pairs if ast.get_docstring(pair[0], clean=False) is None]
72	        if not missing_pairs or any(ast.get_docstring(modified, clean=False) is None for _pristine, modified in missing_pairs):
73	            coverage_missing.append(qualname)
74	    if coverage_missing:
75	        return fail("coverage", "missing inserted docstrings: " + ", ".join(coverage_missing))
76	
77	    stripped_pristine = ast.dump(strip_docstrings(pristine_tree), include_attributes=False)
78	    stripped_modified = ast.dump(strip_docstrings(modified_tree), include_attributes=False)
79	    if stripped_pristine != stripped_modified:
80	        return fail("ast_equivalence", "AST differs after stripping docstrings")
81	
82	    for qualname in item["targets"]:
83	        pairs = list(zip(pristine_nodes.get(qualname, []), modified_nodes.get(qualname, [])))
84	        for pristine_node, modified_node in pairs:
85	            if ast.get_docstring(pristine_node, clean=False) is not None:
86	                continue
87	            docstring = ast.get_docstring(modified_node, clean=False) or ""
88	            if len(docstring.strip()) < 20 or PLACEHOLDER_RE.search(docstring):
89	                return fail("non_placeholder", f"{qualname} docstring is too short or placeholder-like")
90	            if isinstance(pristine_node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef)):
91	                params = params_for(pristine_node)
92	                missing = [name for name in params if not word_present(docstring, name)]
93	                if missing:
94	                    return fail("parameter_mention", f"{qualname} missing parameter mention(s): {', '.join(missing)}")
95	    return ok()
96	
97	
98	def collect_nodes(tree: ast.AST) -> dict[str, ast.AST]:
99	    nodes: dict[str, ast.AST] = {}
100	
101	    def walk_body(body: list[ast.stmt], stack: list[str]) -> None:
102	        for node in body:
103	            if isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef, ast.ClassDef)):
104	                qualname = ".".join([*stack, node.name])
105	                nodes[qualname] = node
106	                walk_body(node.body, [*stack, node.name])
107	
108	    if isinstance(tree, ast.Module):
109	        walk_body(tree.body, [])
110	    return nodes
111	
112	
113	def collect_nodes_multi(tree: ast.AST) -> dict[str, list[ast.AST]]:
114	    nodes: dict[str, list[ast.AST]] = {}
115	
116	    def walk_body(body: list[ast.stmt], stack: list[str]) -> None:
117	        for node in body:
118	            if isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef, ast.ClassDef)):
119	                qualname = ".".join([*stack, node.name])
120	                nodes.setdefault(qualname, []).append(node)
121	                walk_body(node.body, [*stack, node.name])
122	
123	    if isinstance(tree, ast.Module):
124	        walk_body(tree.body, [])
125	    return nodes
126	
127	
128	def main() -> int:
129	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Verify one fan-out item scratch file.")
130	    parser.add_argument("--corpus", required=True)
131	    parser.add_argument("--item", required=True)
132	    parser.add_argument("--modified", required=True)
133	    parser.add_argument("--out", required=True)
134	    parser.add_argument("--insertion-rc", type=int, default=0)
135	    args = parser.parse_args()
136	
137	    item = read_json(pathlib.Path(args.item))
138	    result = verify(pathlib.Path(args.corpus), item, pathlib.Path(args.modified), args.insertion_rc)
139	    write_json(pathlib.Path(args.out), result)
140	    return 0 if result["passed"] else 1
141	
142	
143	if __name__ == "__main__":
144	    raise SystemExit(main())
145

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Insert benchmark docstrings into a scratch copy of one Python file."""
3	
4	from __future__ import annotations
5	
6	import argparse
7	import ast
8	import json
9	import pathlib
10	import sys
11	from typing import Any
12	
13	SCRIPT_DIR = pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parent
14	if str(SCRIPT_DIR) not in sys.path:
15	    sys.path.insert(0, str(SCRIPT_DIR))
16	
17	from fanout_common import item_file, normalize_docstring_keys, parse_source, qualname_targets, read_json  # noqa: E402
18	
19	
20	def load_response(path: pathlib.Path) -> dict[str, str]:
21	    body = read_json(path)
22	    docstrings = body.get("docstrings") if isinstance(body, dict) else None
23	    if not isinstance(docstrings, dict):
24	        raise ValueError("response must contain object key docstrings")
25	    out: dict[str, str] = {}
26	    for key, value in docstrings.items():
27	        if not isinstance(key, str) or not isinstance(value, str):
28	            raise ValueError("docstring map keys and values must be strings")
29	        out[key] = value
30	    return out
31	
32	
33	def insert_docstrings(source: str, item: dict[str, Any], docstrings: dict[str, str]) -> str:
34	    tree = parse_source(source, item["path"])
35	    targets = qualname_targets(tree)
36	    wanted = list(item["targets"])
37	    unknown = sorted(set(wanted) - set(targets))
38	    if unknown:
39	        raise ValueError(f"targets not found without docstrings: {', '.join(unknown)}")
40	    docstrings = normalize_docstring_keys(docstrings, wanted)
41	
42	    lines = source.splitlines(keepends=True)
43	    operations: list[tuple[int, str, str]] = []
44	    named_nodes: dict[str, list[ast.AST]] = {}
45	    for qualname, node in ast_walk_named(parse_source(source, item["path"])):
46	        named_nodes.setdefault(qualname, []).append(node)
47	    for qualname in wanted:
48	        candidate_nodes = [
49	            node
50	            for node in named_nodes.get(qualname, [])
51	            if isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef, ast.ClassDef))
52	            and ast.get_docstring(node, clean=False) is None
53	        ]
54	        if not candidate_nodes:
55	            raise ValueError(f"target not found without docstring: {qualname}")
56	        for node in candidate_nodes:
57	            add_operation(operations, lines, node, qualname, docstrings[qualname])
58	
59	    seen_replacements: set[int] = set()
60	    for line_index, op, text in sorted(operations, key=lambda row: row[0], reverse=True):
61	        if op == "replace":
62	            if line_index in seen_replacements:
63	                raise ValueError(f"multiple one-line suite replacements on line {line_index + 1}")
64	            seen_replacements.add(line_index)
65	            lines[line_index] = text
66	        else:
67	            lines.insert(line_index, text)
68	    return "".join(lines)
69	
70	
71	def add_operation(
72	    operations: list[tuple[int, str, str]],
73	    lines: list[str],
74	    node: ast.FunctionDef | ast.AsyncFunctionDef | ast.ClassDef,
75	    qualname: str,
76	    docstring: str,
77	) -> None:
78	    if not node.body:
79	        raise ValueError(f"target has no body: {qualname}")
80	    first_stmt = node.body[0]
81	    literal = json.dumps(docstring, ensure_ascii=False)
82	    suite_line_index = first_stmt.lineno - 1 if first_stmt.lineno != node.lineno else node.lineno - 1
83	    suite_line = lines[suite_line_index]
84	    suite_text = suite_line[:-1] if suite_line.endswith("\n") else suite_line
85	    suite_colon_index = suite_text.rfind(":", 0, first_stmt.col_offset)
86	    suite_prefix = suite_text[: suite_colon_index + 1].strip() if suite_colon_index != -1 else ""
87	    looks_like_suite_header = suite_prefix.startswith(("def ", "async def ", "class ", ")"))
88	    if first_stmt.lineno == node.lineno or (suite_colon_index != -1 and looks_like_suite_header):
89	        line_index = suite_line_index
90	        original = lines[line_index]
91	        newline = "\n" if original.endswith("\n") else ""
92	        stripped_newline = original[:-1] if newline else original
93	        colon_index = suite_colon_index
94	        if colon_index == -1:
95	            raise ValueError(f"cannot locate one-line suite colon for {qualname}")
96	        block_indent = " " * (node.col_offset + 4)
97	        body_text = stripped_newline[colon_index + 1 :].strip()
98	        if not body_text:
99	            raise ValueError(f"empty one-line suite body for {qualname}")
100	        replacement = (
101	            stripped_newline[: colon_index + 1]
102	            + "\n"
103	            + f"{block_indent}{literal}\n"
104	            + f"{block_indent}{body_text}"
105	            + newline
106	        )
107	        operations.append((line_index, "replace", replacement))
108	        return
109	
110	    if isinstance(node, ast.ClassDef) and isinstance(
111	        first_stmt, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef, ast.ClassDef)
112	    ) and first_stmt.decorator_list:
113	        insertion_line = min(decorator.lineno for decorator in first_stmt.decorator_list) - 1
114	        indent = " " * first_stmt.col_offset
115	    else:
116	        insertion_line = first_stmt.lineno - 1
117	        indent = " " * first_stmt.col_offset
118	    operations.append((insertion_line, "insert", f"{indent}{literal}\n"))
119	
120	
121	def ast_walk_named(tree):
122	    def walk_body(body, stack):
123	        for node in body:
124	            if isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef, ast.ClassDef)):
125	                next_stack = [*stack, node.name]
126	                yield ".".join(next_stack), node
127	                yield from walk_body(node.body, next_stack)
128	
129	    yield from walk_body(tree.body, [])
130	
131	
132	def main() -> int:
133	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Insert docstrings for one fan-out item.")
134	    parser.add_argument("--corpus", required=True)
135	    parser.add_argument("--item", required=True)
136	    parser.add_argument("--response", required=True)
137	    parser.add_argument("--out", required=True)
138	    args = parser.parse_args()
139	
140	    item = read_json(pathlib.Path(args.item))
141	    source_path = item_file(pathlib.Path(args.corpus), item)
142	    source = source_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
143	    docstrings = load_response(pathlib.Path(args.response))
144	    modified = insert_docstrings(source, item, docstrings)
145	    out = pathlib.Path(args.out)
146	    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
147	    out.write_text(modified, encoding="utf-8")
148	    return 0
149	
150	
151	if __name__ == "__main__":
152	    raise SystemExit(main())
153

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Insert benchmark docstrings into a scratch copy of one Python file."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import ast
import json
import pathlib
import sys
from typing import Any

SCRIPT_DIR = pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parent
if str(SCRIPT_DIR) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(SCRIPT_DIR))

from fanout_common import item_file, normalize_docstring_keys, parse_source, qualname_targets, read_json  # noqa: E402


def load_response(path: pathlib.Path) -> dict[str, str]:
    body = read_json(path)
    docstrings = body.get("docstrings") if isinstance(body, dict) else None
    if not isinstance(docstrings, dict):
        raise ValueError("response must contain object key docstrings")
    out: dict[str, str] = {}
    for key, value in docstrings.items():
        if not isinstance(key, str) or not isinstance(value, str):
            raise ValueError("docstring map keys and values must be strings")
        out[key] = value
    return out


def insert_docstrings(source: str, item: dict[str, Any], docstrings: dict[str, str]) -> str:
    tree = parse_source(source, item["path"])
    targets = qualname_targets(tree)
    wanted = list(item["targets"])
    unknown = sorted(set(wanted) - set(targets))
    if unknown:
        raise ValueError(f"targets not found without docstrings: {', '.join(unknown)}")
    docstrings = normalize_docstring_keys(docstrings, wanted)

    lines = source.splitlines(keepends=True)
    operations: list[tuple[int, str, str]] = []
    named_nodes: dict[str, list[ast.AST]] = {}
    for qualname, node in ast_walk_named(parse_source(source, item["path"])):
        named_nodes.setdefault(qualname, []).append(node)
    for qualname in wanted:
        candidate_nodes = [
            node
            for node in named_nodes.get(qualname, [])
            if isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef, ast.ClassDef))
            and ast.get_docstring(node, clean=False) is None
        ]
        if not candidate_nodes:
            raise ValueError(f"target not found without docstring: {qualname}")
        for node in candidate_nodes:
            add_operation(operations, lines, node, qualname, docstrings[qualname])

    seen_replacements: set[int] = set()
    for line_index, op, text in sorted(operations, key=lambda row: row[0], reverse=True):
        if op == "replace":
            if line_index in seen_replacements:
                raise ValueError(f"multiple one-line suite replacements on line {line_index + 1}")
            seen_replacements.add(line_index)
            lines[line_index] = text
        else:
            lines.insert(line_index, text)
    return "".join(lines)


def add_operation(
    operations: list[tuple[int, str, str]],
    lines: list[str],
    node: ast.FunctionDef | ast.AsyncFunctionDef | ast.ClassDef,
    qualname: str,
    docstring: str,
) -> None:
    if not node.body:
        raise ValueError(f"target has no body: {qualname}")
    first_stmt = node.body[0]
    literal = json.dumps(docstring, ensure_ascii=False)
    suite_line_index = first_stmt.lineno - 1 if first_stmt.lineno != node.lineno else node.lineno - 1
    suite_line = lines[suite_line_index]
    suite_text = suite_line[:-1] if suite_line.endswith("\n") else suite_line
    suite_colon_index = suite_text.rfind(":", 0, first_stmt.col_offset)
    suite_prefix = suite_text[: suite_colon_index + 1].strip() if suite_colon_index != -1 else ""
    looks_like_suite_header = suite_prefix.startswith(("def ", "async def ", "class ", ")"))
    if first_stmt.lineno == node.lineno or (suite_colon_index != -1 and looks_like_suite_header):
        line_index = suite_line_index
        original = lines[line_index]
        newline = "\n" if original.endswith("\n") else ""
        stripped_newline = original[:-1] if newline else original
        colon_index = suite_colon_index
        if colon_index == -1:
            raise ValueError(f"cannot locate one-line suite colon for {qualname}")
        block_indent = " " * (node.col_offset + 4)
        body_text = stripped_newline[colon_index + 1 :].strip()
        if not body_text:
            raise ValueError(f"empty one-line suite body for {qualname}")
        replacement = (
            stripped_newline[: colon_index + 1]
            + "\n"
            + f"{block_indent}{literal}\n"
            + f"{block_indent}{body_text}"
            + newline
        )
        operations.append((line_index, "replace", replacement))
        return

    if isinstance(node, ast.ClassDef) and isinstance(
        first_stmt, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef, ast.ClassDef)
    ) and first_stmt.decorator_list:
        insertion_line = min(decorator.lineno for decorator in first_stmt.decorator_list) - 1
        indent = " " * first_stmt.col_offset
    else:
        insertion_line = first_stmt.lineno - 1
        indent = " " * first_stmt.col_offset
    operations.append((insertion_line, "insert", f"{indent}{literal}\n"))


def ast_walk_named(tree):
    def walk_body(body, stack):
        for node in body:
            if isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef, ast.ClassDef)):
                next_stack = [*stack, node.name]
                yield ".".join(next_stack), node
                yield from walk_body(node.body, next_stack)

    yield from walk_body(tree.body, [])


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Insert docstrings for one fan-out item.")
    parser.add_argument("--corpus", required=True)
    parser.add_argument("--item", required=True)
    parser.add_argument("--response", required=True)
    parser.add_argument("--out", required=True)
    args = parser.parse_args()

    item = read_json(pathlib.Path(args.item))
    source_path = item_file(pathlib.Path(args.corpus), item)
    source = source_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    docstrings = load_response(pathlib.Path(args.response))
    modified = insert_docstrings(source, item, docstrings)
    out = pathlib.Path(args.out)
    out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    out.write_text(modified, encoding="utf-8")
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Shared helpers for the showcase fan-out docstring benchmark."""
3	
4	from __future__ import annotations
5	
6	import ast
7	import copy
8	import hashlib
9	import json
10	import pathlib
11	import subprocess
12	from dataclasses import dataclass
13	from datetime import datetime, timezone
14	from typing import Any, Iterable
15	
16	
17	CORPUS_URL = "https://github.com/scrapy/scrapy.git"
18	PINNED_SHA = "a8ffdcf8517a8973391a14635234b6993b15a86a"

20	DEFAULT_BASE_URL = "http://127.0.0.1:8091/v1"
21	
22	
23	@dataclass(frozen=True)
24	class Target:
25	    qualname: str
26	    kind: str
27	    lineno: int
28	    col_offset: int
29	    params: tuple[str, ...]
30	
31	
32	def now_iso() -> str:
33	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).astimezone().isoformat(timespec="milliseconds")
34	
35	
36	def sha256_text(text: str) -> str:
37	    return hashlib.sha256(text.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
38	
39	
40	def read_json(path: pathlib.Path) -> Any:
41	    return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
42	
43	
44	def write_json(path: pathlib.Path, value: Any) -> None:
45	    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
46	    path.write_text(json.dumps(value, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
47	
48	
49	def repo_head(corpus: pathlib.Path) -> str:
50	    result = subprocess.run(
51	        ["git", "-C", str(corpus), "rev-parse", "HEAD"],
52	        capture_output=True,
53	        text=True,
54	        check=True,
55	    )
56	    return result.stdout.strip()
57	
58	
59	def verify_corpus_head(corpus: pathlib.Path) -> None:
60	    head = repo_head(corpus)
61	    if head != PINNED_SHA:
62	        raise SystemExit(f"corpus HEAD {head} != pinned {PINNED_SHA}")
63	
64	
65	def generator_git_sha(repo_root: pathlib.Path) -> str | None:
66	    result = subprocess.run(
67	        ["git", "-C", str(repo_root), "rev-parse", "HEAD"],
68	        capture_output=True,
69	        text=True,
70	    )
71	    return result.stdout.strip() if result.returncode == 0 else None
72	
73	
74	def public_name(name: str) -> bool:
75	    return not name.startswith("_")
76	
77	
78	def params_for(node: ast.FunctionDef | ast.AsyncFunctionDef) -> tuple[str, ...]:
79	    args = node.args
80	    names: list[str] = []
81	    names.extend(arg.arg for arg in args.posonlyargs)
82	    names.extend(arg.arg for arg in args.args)
83	    if args.vararg is not None:
84	        names.append(args.vararg.arg)
85	    names.extend(arg.arg for arg in args.kwonlyargs)
86	    if args.kwarg is not None:
87	        names.append(args.kwarg.arg)
88	    return tuple(name for name in names if name not in {"self", "cls"})
89	
90	
91	def qualname_targets(tree: ast.AST) -> dict[str, Target]:
92	    targets: dict[str, Target] = {}
93	
94	    def visit_body(body: Iterable[ast.stmt], stack: list[str]) -> None:
95	        for node in body:
96	            if isinstance(node, (ast.FunctionDef, ast.AsyncFunctionDef, ast.ClassDef)):
97	                qualname = ".".join([*stack, node.name])
98	                if public_name(node.name) and ast.get_docstring(node, clean=False) is None:
99	                    if isinstance(node, ast.ClassDef):
100	                        targets[qualname] = Target(qualname, "class", node.lineno, node.col_offset, ())
101	                    else:
102	                        targets[qualname] = Target(
103	                            qualname,
104	                            "function",
105	                            node.lineno,
106	                            node.col_offset,
107	                            params_for(node),
108	                        )
109	                visit_body(node.body, [*stack, node.name])
110	
111	    if not isinstance(tree, ast.Module):
112	        return targets
113	    visit_body(tree.body, [])
114	    return targets
115	
116	
117	def parse_source(source: str, filename: str = "<source>") -> ast.Module:
118	    return ast.parse(source, filename=filename)
119	
120	
121	def item_file(corpus: pathlib.Path, item: dict[str, Any]) -> pathlib.Path:
122	    rel = pathlib.PurePosixPath(item["path"])
123	    if rel.is_absolute() or ".." in rel.parts:
124	        raise ValueError(f"unsafe item path: {item['path']}")
125	    path = (corpus / pathlib.Path(*rel.parts)).resolve()
126	    corpus_resolved = corpus.resolve()
127	    if path != corpus_resolved and corpus_resolved not in path.parents:
128	        raise ValueError(f"item path escapes corpus: {item['path']}")
129	    return path
130	
131	
132	def strip_docstrings(tree: ast.AST) -> ast.AST:
133	    class Stripper(ast.NodeTransformer):
134	        def strip_body(self, node: ast.Module | ast.ClassDef | ast.FunctionDef | ast.AsyncFunctionDef):
135	            self.generic_visit(node)
136	            if node.body and isinstance(node.body[0], ast.Expr):
137	                value = node.body[0].value
138	                if isinstance(value, ast.Constant) and isinstance(value.value, str):
139	                    node.body = node.body[1:]
140	            return node
141	
142	        def visit_Module(self, node: ast.Module):
143	            return self.strip_body(node)
144	
145	        def visit_ClassDef(self, node: ast.ClassDef):
146	            return self.strip_body(node)
147	
148	        def visit_FunctionDef(self, node: ast.FunctionDef):
149	            return self.strip_body(node)
150	
151	        def visit_AsyncFunctionDef(self, node: ast.AsyncFunctionDef):
152	            return self.strip_body(node)
153	
154	    return ast.fix_missing_locations(Stripper().visit(copy.deepcopy(tree)))
155	
156	
157	def sorted_item_paths(items_dir: pathlib.Path) -> list[pathlib.Path]:
158	    return sorted(items_dir.glob("item-*.json"))
159	
160	
161	def v1_url(base: str, path: str) -> str:
162	    base = base.rstrip("/")
163	    if base.endswith("/v1"):
164	        return base + path
165	    return base + "/v1" + path
166	
167	
168	def extract_json_object(text: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
169	    try:
170	        parsed = json.loads(text)
171	    except json.JSONDecodeError:
172	        parsed = json.loads(first_balanced_json_object(text))
173	    if not isinstance(parsed, dict):
174	        raise ValueError("assistant response is not a JSON object")
175	    docstrings = parsed.get("docstrings")
176	    if (
177	        docstrings is None
178	        and parsed
179	        and all(isinstance(key, str) and isinstance(value, str) for key, value in parsed.items())
180	    ):
181	        docstrings = parsed
182	    if not isinstance(docstrings, dict):
183	        raise ValueError("assistant response missing docstrings object")
184	    for key, value in docstrings.items():
185	        if not isinstance(key, str) or not isinstance(value, str):
186	            raise ValueError("docstrings must map strings to strings")
187	    return {"docstrings": docstrings}
188	
189	
190	def first_balanced_json_object(text: str) -> str:
191	    start = text.find("{")
192	    if start == -1:
193	        raise json.JSONDecodeError("no JSON object found", text, 0)
194	
195	    depth = 0
196	    in_string = False
197	    escaped = False
198	    for index in range(start, len(text)):
199	        char = text[index]
200	        if in_string:
201	            if escaped:
202	                escaped = False
203	            elif char == "\\":
204	                escaped = True
205	            elif char == '"':
206	                in_string = False
207	            continue
208	        if char == '"':
209	            in_string = True
210	        elif char == "{":
211	            depth += 1
212	        elif char == "}":
213	            depth -= 1
214	            if depth == 0:
215	                return text[start : index + 1]
216	    raise json.JSONDecodeError("unterminated JSON object", text, start)
217	
218	
219	def normalize_docstring_keys(docstrings: dict[str, str], expected: Iterable[str]) -> dict[str, str]:
220	    expected_list = list(expected)
221	    expected_set = set(expected_list)
222	    normalized: dict[str, str] = {}
223	    source_keys: dict[str, str] = {}
224	
225	    for key, value in docstrings.items():
226	        if key in expected_set:
227	            matches = [key]
228	        else:
229	            matches = [qualname for qualname in expected_list if key.endswith("." + qualname)]
230	        if not matches:
231	            raise ValueError(f"extra docstrings for: {key}")
232	        if len(matches) > 1:
233	            raise ValueError(
234	                f"ambiguous docstring key {key!r} matches: {', '.join(sorted(matches))}"
235	            )
236	        qualname = matches[0]
237	        if qualname in normalized:
238	            raise ValueError(
239	                f"ambiguous docstring keys for {qualname}: {source_keys[qualname]!r}, {key!r}"
240	            )
241	        normalized[qualname] = value
242	        source_keys[qualname] = key
243	
244	    missing = sorted(expected_set - set(normalized))
245	    if missing:
246	        raise ValueError(f"missing docstrings for: {', '.join(missing)}")
247	    return normalized
248	
249	
250	def write_tokens(out: pathlib.Path, responses: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> None:
251	    total_prompt = 0
252	    total_completion = 0
253	    requests = []
254	    for i, body in enumerate(responses, 1):
255	        usage = body.get("usage") or {}
256	        prompt = usage.get("prompt_tokens") or 0
257	        completion = usage.get("completion_tokens") or 0
258	        total_prompt += prompt
259	        total_completion += completion
260	        requests.append(
261	            {
262	                "request_index": i,
263	                "prompt_tokens": prompt,
264	                "completion_tokens": completion,
265	                "client_wall_s": body.get("_client_wall_s"),
266	                "timings": body.get("timings") or None,
267	            }
268	        )
269	    write_json(
270	        out / "tokens.json",
271	        {
272	            "prompt_tokens": total_prompt,
273	            "completion_tokens": total_completion,
274	            "total_tokens": total_prompt + total_completion,
275	            "requests": requests,
276	        },
277	    )

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import re
4	import time
5	from http.cookiejar import Cookie, CookiePolicy, DefaultCookiePolicy
6	from http.cookiejar import CookieJar as _CookieJar
7	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, cast
8	
9	from scrapy.utils.httpobj import urlparse_cached
10	from scrapy.utils.python import to_unicode
11	
12	if TYPE_CHECKING:
13	    from collections.abc import Iterator, Sequence
14	
15	    # typing.Self requires Python 3.11
16	    from typing_extensions import Self
17	
18	    from scrapy import Request
19	    from scrapy.http import Response
20	
21	
22	# Defined in the http.cookiejar module, but undocumented:
23	# https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/v3.9.0/Lib/http/cookiejar.py#L527
24	IPV4_RE = re.compile(r"\.\d+$", re.ASCII)
25	
26	
27	class CookieJar:
28	    def __init__(
29	        self,
30	        policy: CookiePolicy | None = None,
31	        check_expired_frequency: int = 10000,
32	    ):
33	        self.policy: CookiePolicy = policy or DefaultCookiePolicy()
34	        self.jar: _CookieJar = _CookieJar(self.policy)
35	        self.jar._cookies_lock = _DummyLock()  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
36	        self.check_expired_frequency: int = check_expired_frequency
37	        self.processed: int = 0
38	
39	    def extract_cookies(self, response: Response, request: Request) -> None:
40	        wreq = WrappedRequest(request)
41	        wrsp = WrappedResponse(response)
42	        self.jar.extract_cookies(wrsp, wreq)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
43	
44	    def add_cookie_header(self, request: Request) -> None:
45	        wreq = WrappedRequest(request)
46	        self.policy._now = self.jar._now = int(time.time())  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
47	
48	        # the cookiejar implementation iterates through all domains
49	        # instead we restrict to potential matches on the domain
50	        req_host = urlparse_cached(request).hostname
51	        if not req_host:
52	            return
53	
54	        if not IPV4_RE.search(req_host):
55	            hosts = potential_domain_matches(req_host)
56	            if "." not in req_host:
57	                hosts.append(req_host + ".local")
58	        else:
59	            hosts = [req_host]
60	
61	        cookies = []
62	        for host in hosts:
63	            if host in self.jar._cookies:  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
64	                cookies.extend(self.jar._cookies_for_domain(host, wreq))  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
65	
66	        attrs = self.jar._cookie_attrs(cookies)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
67	        if attrs and not wreq.has_header("Cookie"):
68	            wreq.add_unredirected_header("Cookie", "; ".join(attrs))
69	
70	        self.processed += 1
71	        if self.processed % self.check_expired_frequency == 0:
72	            # This is still quite inefficient for large number of cookies
73	            self.jar.clear_expired_cookies()
74	
75	    @property
76	    def _cookies(self) -> dict[str, dict[str, dict[str, Cookie]]]:
77	        return self.jar._cookies  # type: ignore[attr-defined,no-any-return]
78	
79	    def clear_session_cookies(self) -> None:
80	        return self.jar.clear_session_cookies()
81	
82	    def clear(
83	        self,
84	        domain: str | None = None,
85	        path: str | None = None,
86	        name: str | None = None,
87	    ) -> None:
88	        self.jar.clear(domain, path, name)
89	
90	    def __iter__(self) -> Iterator[Cookie]:
91	        return iter(self.jar)
92	
93	    def __len__(self) -> int:
94	        return len(self.jar)
95	
96	    def set_policy(self, pol: CookiePolicy) -> None:
97	        self.jar.set_policy(pol)
98	
99	    def make_cookies(self, response: Response, request: Request) -> Sequence[Cookie]:
100	        wreq = WrappedRequest(request)
101	        wrsp = WrappedResponse(response)
102	        return self.jar.make_cookies(wrsp, wreq)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
103	
104	    def set_cookie(self, cookie: Cookie) -> None:
105	        self.jar.set_cookie(cookie)
106	
107	    def set_cookie_if_ok(self, cookie: Cookie, request: Request) -> None:
108	        self.jar.set_cookie_if_ok(cookie, WrappedRequest(request))  # type: ignore[arg-type]
109	
110	
111	def potential_domain_matches(domain: str) -> list[str]:
112	    """Potential domain matches for a cookie
113	
114	    >>> potential_domain_matches('www.example.com')
115	    ['www.example.com', 'example.com', '.www.example.com', '.example.com']
116	
117	    """
118	    matches = [domain]
119	    try:
120	        start = domain.index(".") + 1
121	        end = domain.rindex(".")
122	        while start < end:
123	            matches.append(domain[start:])
124	            start = domain.index(".", start) + 1
125	    except ValueError:
126	        pass
127	    return matches + ["." + d for d in matches]
128	
129	
130	class _DummyLock:
131	    def acquire(self) -> None:
132	        pass
133	
134	    def release(self) -> None:
135	        pass
136	
137	
138	class WrappedRequest:
139	    """Wraps a scrapy Request class with methods defined by urllib2.Request class to interact with CookieJar class
140	
141	    see http://docs.python.org/library/urllib2.html#urllib2.Request
142	    """
143	
144	    def __init__(self, request: Request):
145	        self.request = request
146	
147	    def get_full_url(self) -> str:
148	        return self.request.url
149	
150	    def get_host(self) -> str:
151	        return urlparse_cached(self.request).netloc
152	
153	    def get_type(self) -> str:
154	        return urlparse_cached(self.request).scheme
155	
156	    def is_unverifiable(self) -> bool:
157	        """Unverifiable should indicate whether the request is unverifiable, as defined by RFC 2965.
158	
159	        It defaults to False. An unverifiable request is one whose URL the user did not have the
160	        option to approve. For example, if the request is for an image in an
161	        HTML document, and the user had no option to approve the automatic
162	        fetching of the image, this should be true.
163	        """
164	        return cast("bool", self.request.meta.get("is_unverifiable", False))
165	
166	    @property
167	    def full_url(self) -> str:
168	        return self.get_full_url()
169	
170	    @property
171	    def host(self) -> str:
172	        return self.get_host()
173	
174	    @property
175	    def type(self) -> str:
176	        return self.get_type()
177	
178	    @property
179	    def unverifiable(self) -> bool:
180	        return self.is_unverifiable()
181	
182	    @property
183	    def origin_req_host(self) -> str:
184	        return cast("str", urlparse_cached(self.request).hostname)
185	
186	    def has_header(self, name: str) -> bool:
187	        return name in self.request.headers
188	
189	    def get_header(self, name: str, default: str | None = None) -> str | None:
190	        value = self.request.headers.get(name, default)
191	        return to_unicode(value, errors="replace") if value is not None else None
192	
193	    def header_items(self) -> list[tuple[str, list[str]]]:
194	        return [
195	            (
196	                to_unicode(k, errors="replace"),
197	                [to_unicode(x, errors="replace") for x in v],
198	            )
199	            for k, v in self.request.headers.items()
200	        ]
201	
202	    def add_unredirected_header(self, name: str, value: str) -> None:
203	        self.request.headers.appendlist(name, value)
204	
205	
206	class WrappedResponse:
207	    def __init__(self, response: Response):
208	        self.response = response
209	
210	    def info(self) -> Self:
211	        return self
212	
213	    def get_all(self, name: str, default: Any = None) -> list[str]:
214	        return [
215	            to_unicode(v, errors="replace") for v in self.response.headers.getlist(name)
216	        ]
217

1	"""
2	Scrapy extension for collecting scraping stats
3	"""
4	
5	from __future__ import annotations
6	
7	import logging
8	import pprint
9	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
10	
11	from scrapy.utils.decorators import _warn_spider_arg
12	
13	if TYPE_CHECKING:
14	    from scrapy import Spider
15	    from scrapy.crawler import Crawler
16	
17	
18	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
19	
20	
21	StatsT = dict[str, Any]
22	
23	
24	class StatsCollector:
25	    def __init__(self, crawler: Crawler):
26	        self._dump: bool = crawler.settings.getbool("STATS_DUMP")
27	        self._stats: StatsT = {}
28	        self._crawler: Crawler = crawler
29	
30	    def __getattribute__(self, name: str) -> Any:
31	        cached_name = f"_cached_{name}"
32	        try:
33	            return super().__getattribute__(cached_name)
34	        except AttributeError:
35	            pass
36	
37	        original_attr = super().__getattribute__(name)
38	
39	        if name in {
40	            "get_value",
41	            "get_stats",
42	            "set_value",
43	            "set_stats",
44	            "inc_value",
45	            "max_value",
46	            "min_value",
47	            "clear_stats",
48	            "open_spider",
49	            "close_spider",
50	        } and callable(original_attr):
51	            wrapped = _warn_spider_arg(original_attr)
52	            setattr(self, cached_name, wrapped)
53	            return wrapped
54	
55	        return original_attr
56	
57	    def get_value(
58	        self, key: str, default: Any = None, spider: Spider | None = None
59	    ) -> Any:
60	        return self._stats.get(key, default)
61	
62	    def get_stats(self, spider: Spider | None = None) -> StatsT:
63	        return self._stats
64	
65	    def set_value(self, key: str, value: Any, spider: Spider | None = None) -> None:
66	        self._stats[key] = value
67	
68	    def set_stats(self, stats: StatsT, spider: Spider | None = None) -> None:
69	        self._stats = stats
70	
71	    def inc_value(
72	        self, key: str, count: int = 1, start: int = 0, spider: Spider | None = None
73	    ) -> None:
74	        d = self._stats
75	        d[key] = d.setdefault(key, start) + count
76	
77	    def max_value(self, key: str, value: Any, spider: Spider | None = None) -> None:
78	        self._stats[key] = max(self._stats.setdefault(key, value), value)
79	
80	    def min_value(self, key: str, value: Any, spider: Spider | None = None) -> None:
81	        self._stats[key] = min(self._stats.setdefault(key, value), value)
82	
83	    def clear_stats(self, spider: Spider | None = None) -> None:
84	        self._stats.clear()
85	
86	    def open_spider(self, spider: Spider | None = None) -> None:
87	        pass
88	
89	    def close_spider(
90	        self, spider: Spider | None = None, reason: str | None = None
91	    ) -> None:
92	        if self._dump:
93	            logger.info(
94	                "Dumping Scrapy stats:\n" + pprint.pformat(self._stats),
95	                extra={"spider": self._crawler.spider},
96	            )
97	        self._persist_stats(self._stats)
98	
99	    def _persist_stats(self, stats: StatsT) -> None:
100	        pass
101	
102	
103	class MemoryStatsCollector(StatsCollector):
104	    def __init__(self, crawler: Crawler):
105	        super().__init__(crawler)
106	        self.spider_stats: dict[str, StatsT] = {}
107	
108	    def _persist_stats(self, stats: StatsT) -> None:
109	        if self._crawler.spider:
110	            self.spider_stats[self._crawler.spider.name] = stats
111	
112	
113	class DummyStatsCollector(StatsCollector):
114	    def get_value(
115	        self, key: str, default: Any = None, spider: Spider | None = None
116	    ) -> Any:
117	        return default
118	
119	    def set_value(self, key: str, value: Any, spider: Spider | None = None) -> None:
120	        pass
121	
122	    def set_stats(self, stats: StatsT, spider: Spider | None = None) -> None:
123	        pass
124	
125	    def inc_value(
126	        self, key: str, count: int = 1, start: int = 0, spider: Spider | None = None
127	    ) -> None:
128	        pass
129	
130	    def max_value(self, key: str, value: Any, spider: Spider | None = None) -> None:
131	        pass
132	
133	    def min_value(self, key: str, value: Any, spider: Spider | None = None) -> None:
134	        pass
135

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import re
4	import sys
5	from collections.abc import AsyncGenerator, Iterable
6	from functools import wraps
7	from inspect import getmembers
8	from types import CoroutineType
9	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, ClassVar, cast
10	from unittest import TestCase, TestResult
11	
12	from scrapy.http import Request, Response
13	from scrapy.utils.python import get_spec
14	from scrapy.utils.spider import iterate_spider_output
15	
16	if TYPE_CHECKING:
17	    from collections.abc import Callable
18	
19	    from twisted.python.failure import Failure
20	
21	    from scrapy import Spider
22	
23	
24	class Contract:
25	    """Abstract class for contracts"""
26	
27	    request_cls: type[Request] | None = None
28	    name: str
29	
30	    def __init__(self, method: Callable[..., Any], *args: Any):
31	        self.testcase_pre = _create_testcase(method, f"@{self.name} pre-hook")
32	        self.testcase_post = _create_testcase(method, f"@{self.name} post-hook")
33	        self.args: tuple[Any, ...] = args
34	
35	    def add_pre_hook(self, request: Request, results: TestResult) -> Request:
36	        if hasattr(self, "pre_process"):
37	            cb = request.callback
38	            assert cb is not None
39	
40	            @wraps(cb)
41	            def wrapper(response: Response, **cb_kwargs: Any) -> list[Any]:
42	                try:
43	                    results.startTest(self.testcase_pre)
44	                    self.pre_process(response)
45	                    results.stopTest(self.testcase_pre)
46	                except AssertionError:
47	                    results.addFailure(self.testcase_pre, sys.exc_info())
48	                except Exception:
49	                    results.addError(self.testcase_pre, sys.exc_info())
50	                else:
51	                    results.addSuccess(self.testcase_pre)
52	                cb_result = cb(response, **cb_kwargs)
53	                if isinstance(cb_result, (AsyncGenerator, CoroutineType)):
54	                    raise TypeError("Contracts don't support async callbacks")
55	                return list(cast("Iterable[Any]", iterate_spider_output(cb_result)))
56	
57	            request.callback = wrapper
58	
59	        return request
60	
61	    def add_post_hook(self, request: Request, results: TestResult) -> Request:
62	        if hasattr(self, "post_process"):
63	            cb = request.callback
64	            assert cb is not None
65	
66	            @wraps(cb)
67	            def wrapper(response: Response, **cb_kwargs: Any) -> list[Any]:
68	                cb_result = cb(response, **cb_kwargs)
69	                if isinstance(cb_result, (AsyncGenerator, CoroutineType)):
70	                    raise TypeError("Contracts don't support async callbacks")
71	                output = list(cast("Iterable[Any]", iterate_spider_output(cb_result)))
72	                try:
73	                    results.startTest(self.testcase_post)
74	                    self.post_process(output)
75	                    results.stopTest(self.testcase_post)
76	                except AssertionError:
77	                    results.addFailure(self.testcase_post, sys.exc_info())
78	                except Exception:
79	                    results.addError(self.testcase_post, sys.exc_info())
80	                else:
81	                    results.addSuccess(self.testcase_post)
82	                return output
83	
84	            request.callback = wrapper
85	
86	        return request
87	
88	    def adjust_request_args(self, args: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
89	        return args
90	
91	
92	class ContractsManager:
93	    contracts: ClassVar[dict[str, type[Contract]]] = {}
94	
95	    def __init__(self, contracts: Iterable[type[Contract]]):
96	        for contract in contracts:
97	            self.contracts[contract.name] = contract
98	
99	    def tested_methods_from_spidercls(self, spidercls: type[Spider]) -> list[str]:
100	        is_method = re.compile(r"^\s*@", re.MULTILINE).search
101	        methods = []
102	        for key, value in getmembers(spidercls):
103	            if callable(value) and value.__doc__ and is_method(value.__doc__):
104	                methods.append(key)
105	
106	        return methods
107	
108	    def extract_contracts(self, method: Callable[..., Any]) -> list[Contract]:
109	        contracts: list[Contract] = []
110	        assert method.__doc__ is not None
111	        for line_ in method.__doc__.split("\n"):
112	            line = line_.strip()
113	
114	            if line.startswith("@"):
115	                m = re.match(r"@(\w+)\s*(.*)", line)
116	                if m is None:
117	                    continue
118	                name, args = m.groups()
119	                args = re.split(r"\s+", args)
120	
121	                contracts.append(self.contracts[name](method, *args))
122	
123	        return contracts
124	
125	    def from_spider(self, spider: Spider, results: TestResult) -> list[Request | None]:
126	        requests: list[Request | None] = []
127	        for method in self.tested_methods_from_spidercls(type(spider)):
128	            bound_method = getattr(spider, method)
129	            try:
130	                requests.append(self.from_method(bound_method, results))
131	            except Exception:
132	                case = _create_testcase(bound_method, "contract")
133	                results.addError(case, sys.exc_info())
134	
135	        return requests
136	
137	    def from_method(
138	        self, method: Callable[..., Any], results: TestResult
139	    ) -> Request | None:
140	        contracts = self.extract_contracts(method)
141	        if contracts:
142	            request_cls = Request
143	            for contract in contracts:
144	                if contract.request_cls is not None:
145	                    request_cls = contract.request_cls
146	
147	            # calculate request args
148	            args, kwargs = get_spec(request_cls.__init__)
149	
150	            # Don't filter requests to allow
151	            # testing different callbacks on the same URL.
152	            kwargs["dont_filter"] = True
153	            kwargs["callback"] = method
154	
155	            for contract in contracts:
156	                kwargs = contract.adjust_request_args(kwargs)
157	
158	            args.remove("self")
159	
160	            # check if all positional arguments are defined in kwargs
161	            if set(args).issubset(set(kwargs)):
162	                request = request_cls(**kwargs)
163	
164	                # execute pre and post hooks in order
165	                for contract in reversed(contracts):
166	                    request = contract.add_pre_hook(request, results)
167	                for contract in contracts:
168	                    request = contract.add_post_hook(request, results)
169	
170	                self._clean_req(request, method, results)
171	                return request
172	        return None
173	
174	    def _clean_req(
175	        self, request: Request, method: Callable[..., Any], results: TestResult
176	    ) -> None:
177	        """stop the request from returning objects and records any errors"""
178	
179	        cb = request.callback
180	        assert cb is not None
181	
182	        @wraps(cb)
183	        def cb_wrapper(response: Response, **cb_kwargs: Any) -> None:
184	            try:
185	                output = cb(response, **cb_kwargs)
186	                output = list(cast("Iterable[Any]", iterate_spider_output(output)))
187	            except Exception:
188	                case = _create_testcase(method, "callback")
189	                results.addError(case, sys.exc_info())
190	
191	        def eb_wrapper(failure: Failure) -> None:
192	            case = _create_testcase(method, "errback")
193	            exc_info = failure.type, failure.value, failure.getTracebackObject()
194	            results.addError(case, exc_info)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
195	
196	        request.callback = cb_wrapper
197	        request.errback = eb_wrapper
198	
199	
200	def _create_testcase(method: Callable[..., Any], desc: str) -> TestCase:
201	    spider = method.__self__.name  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
202	
203	    class ContractTestCase(TestCase):
204	        def __str__(_self) -> str:  # pylint: disable=no-self-argument
205	            return f"[{spider}] {method.__name__} ({desc})"
206	
207	    name = f"{spider}_{method.__name__}"
208	    setattr(ContractTestCase, name, lambda x: x)
209	    return ContractTestCase(name)
210

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
4	
5	from twisted.internet import defer
6	from twisted.internet.base import ReactorBase, ThreadedResolver
7	from twisted.internet.interfaces import (
8	    IAddress,
9	    IHostnameResolver,
10	    IHostResolution,
11	    IResolutionReceiver,
12	    IResolverSimple,
13	)
14	from zope.interface.declarations import implementer, provider
15	
16	from scrapy.utils.datatypes import LocalCache
17	
18	if TYPE_CHECKING:
19	    from collections.abc import Sequence
20	
21	    from twisted.internet.defer import Deferred
22	
23	    # typing.Self requires Python 3.11
24	    from typing_extensions import Self
25	
26	    from scrapy.crawler import Crawler
27	
28	# TODO: cache misses
29	dnscache: LocalCache[str, Any] = LocalCache(10000)
30	
31	
32	@implementer(IResolverSimple)
33	class CachingThreadedResolver(ThreadedResolver):
34	    """
35	    Default caching resolver. IPv4 only, supports setting a timeout value for DNS requests.
36	    """
37	
38	    def __init__(self, reactor: ReactorBase, cache_size: int, timeout: float):
39	        super().__init__(reactor)
40	        dnscache.limit = cache_size
41	        self.timeout = timeout
42	
43	    @classmethod
44	    def from_crawler(cls, crawler: Crawler, reactor: ReactorBase) -> Self:
45	        if crawler.settings.getbool("DNSCACHE_ENABLED"):
46	            cache_size = crawler.settings.getint("DNSCACHE_SIZE")
47	        else:
48	            cache_size = 0
49	        return cls(reactor, cache_size, crawler.settings.getfloat("DNS_TIMEOUT"))
50	
51	    def install_on_reactor(self) -> None:
52	        self.reactor.installResolver(self)
53	
54	    def getHostByName(self, name: str, timeout: Sequence[int] = ()) -> Deferred[str]:
55	        if name in dnscache:
56	            return defer.succeed(dnscache[name])
57	        # in Twisted<=16.6, getHostByName() is always called with
58	        # a default timeout of 60s (actually passed as (1, 3, 11, 45) tuple),
59	        # so the input argument above is simply overridden
60	        # to enforce Scrapy's DNS_TIMEOUT setting's value
61	        # The timeout arg is typed as Sequence[int] but supports floats.
62	        timeout = (self.timeout,)  # type: ignore[assignment]
63	        d = super().getHostByName(name, timeout)
64	        if dnscache.limit:
65	            d.addCallback(self._cache_result, name)
66	        return d
67	
68	    def _cache_result(self, result: Any, name: str) -> Any:
69	        dnscache[name] = result
70	        return result
71	
72	
73	@implementer(IHostResolution)
74	class HostResolution:
75	    def __init__(self, name: str):
76	        self.name: str = name
77	
78	    def cancel(self) -> None:
79	        raise NotImplementedError
80	
81	
82	@provider(IResolutionReceiver)
83	class _CachingResolutionReceiver:
84	    def __init__(self, resolutionReceiver: IResolutionReceiver, hostName: str):
85	        self.resolutionReceiver: IResolutionReceiver = resolutionReceiver
86	        self.hostName: str = hostName
87	        self.addresses: list[IAddress] = []
88	
89	    def resolutionBegan(self, resolution: IHostResolution) -> None:
90	        self.resolutionReceiver.resolutionBegan(resolution)
91	        self.resolution = resolution
92	
93	    def addressResolved(self, address: IAddress) -> None:
94	        self.resolutionReceiver.addressResolved(address)
95	        self.addresses.append(address)
96	
97	    def resolutionComplete(self) -> None:
98	        self.resolutionReceiver.resolutionComplete()
99	        if self.addresses:
100	            dnscache[self.hostName] = self.addresses
101	
102	
103	@implementer(IHostnameResolver)
104	class CachingHostnameResolver:
105	    """
106	    Experimental caching resolver. Resolves IPv4 and IPv6 addresses,
107	    does not support setting a timeout value for DNS requests.
108	    """
109	
110	    def __init__(self, reactor: ReactorBase, cache_size: int):
111	        self.reactor: ReactorBase = reactor
112	        self.original_resolver: IHostnameResolver = reactor.nameResolver
113	        dnscache.limit = cache_size
114	
115	    @classmethod
116	    def from_crawler(cls, crawler: Crawler, reactor: ReactorBase) -> Self:
117	        if crawler.settings.getbool("DNSCACHE_ENABLED"):
118	            cache_size = crawler.settings.getint("DNSCACHE_SIZE")
119	        else:
120	            cache_size = 0
121	        return cls(reactor, cache_size)
122	
123	    def install_on_reactor(self) -> None:
124	        self.reactor.installNameResolver(self)
125	
126	    def resolveHostName(
127	        self,
128	        resolutionReceiver: IResolutionReceiver,
129	        hostName: str,
130	        portNumber: int = 0,
131	        addressTypes: Sequence[type[IAddress]] | None = None,
132	        transportSemantics: str = "TCP",
133	    ) -> IHostResolution:
134	        try:
135	            addresses = dnscache[hostName]
136	        except KeyError:
137	            return self.original_resolver.resolveHostName(
138	                _CachingResolutionReceiver(resolutionReceiver, hostName),
139	                hostName,
140	                portNumber,
141	                addressTypes,
142	                transportSemantics,
143	            )
144	        resolutionReceiver.resolutionBegan(HostResolution(hostName))
145	        for addr in addresses:
146	            resolutionReceiver.addressResolved(addr)
147	        resolutionReceiver.resolutionComplete()
148	        return resolutionReceiver
149
